chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يَسْتَأْجِرُ الْأَرْضَ أَوِ الدَّارَ فَيُؤَاجِرُهَا بِأَكْثَرَ مِمَّا اسْتَأْجَرَهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 131 – The man leases the land or the house, so he sub-leases it out with more than what he had leased it for

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/355)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/355)
text: الرَّجُلِ يَسْتَأْجِرُ الْأَرْضَ ثُمَّ يُؤَاجِرُهَا بِأَكْثَرَ مِمَّا اسْتَأْجَرَهَا فَقَالَ لَا بَأْسَ إِنَّ هَذَا لَيْسَ كَالْحَانُوتِ وَ لَا الْأَجِيرِ إِنَّ فَضْلَ الْأَجِيرِ وَ الْحَانُوتِ حَرَامٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn AbuUmeyr, from Abu Al Magra,

from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the man who leases a land, then he sub-leases it out with more than what he had leased it for. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem. This is not like the shops, nor like the employees (wages). The excess of the wages and the shops is Prohibited’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from abu al-Mighra’ who has said the following: “About the case of a man who leases a piece of land, then rents it to someone for more than what he has leased for, he (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, said, ‘It is not unlawful because it is not like a shop or wages; increase of wages and rent of shop is not lawful.’”
